# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.

Prevent cloud compute security.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
- name: projects/myprojectid
  type: cloud_deployment_manager.v1.DeploymentManager
  properties:
    targetProjects: ['default'] # replace with your project id here, default is the global one if not specified otherwise it will be used by Deployment Manager itself to create resources in all projects that are targeted or referenced within this deployment manager template instance (unless a different value has been explicitly set). 
  
    sourceType: text-simple // Use 'file' for YAML file. This is the type of resource you want from Google Cloud, such as compute/instance templates etc... For example `cloud_sql` or custom resources defined in another template (or a local JSON object) can also be used here
    sourceUri: "path-to-your-template" // replace with your file path and name. 
  
# The following is an empty resource for the deployment manager to create instances from our templates, it's not actually part of any template itself but rather a placeholder that will get created by Deployment Manager when needed (as specified in sourceUri).   
- type: gcp/compute/instance
  name: projects/myprojectid/instances/default   # replace with your instance id here. This is the unique identifier for this resource within its parent project and region, it should be globally唯一的 to avoid any conflicts when creating resources in GCP (unless a different value has been explicitly set).   
  properties:    
      zone: us-central1   # replace with your instance's desired location. This is the geographical area where this resource will exist and also it determines what type of hardware to use for instances within that region, such as 'us-west2', etc... If not specified then default value (zone) used by GCP Deployment Manager itself
      machineType: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{PROJECT_ID}/zones/{ZONE}/machineTypes/n1-standard-1  # replace with your instance's desired hardware configuration, such as 'https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{PROJECT_ID}/zones/{ZONE}/machineTypes/n1-standard-1', etc... If not specified then default value (zone) used by GCP Deployment Manager itself
      disks:  # replace with your instance's disk configuration, such as 'https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{PROJECT_ID}/zones/{ZONE}/disks/{DISK}', etc... If not specified then default value (zone) used by GCP Deployment Manager itself
      networkInterfaces:  # replace with your instance's desired networking configuration, such as 'https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{PROJECT_ID}/global/networks/{NETWORK}', etc... If not specified then default value (zone) used by GCP Deployment Manager itself
      serviceAccount: https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/projects/{PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/{SERVICE_ACCOUNT},  # replace with your instance's desired identity, such as 'https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/projects/{PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/{SERVICE_ACCOUNT}', etc... If not specified then default value (zone) used by GCP Deployment Manager itself
